name: Arévalo River Walk
name_original: Castillo de Arévalo y Ribera Río Arevalillo — circular
description: This easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) circular hike in Arévalo offers riverside scenery and unique views of the historic Castillo de Arévalo.
region_intro: Hiking around Arévalo offers trails characterized by gentle terrain and minimal elevation changes, making them suitable for various fitness levels. The region features river paths and routes connecting local towns, often passing through open landscapes. These trails provide opportunities to explore the area's natural and rural environments. Best hiking trails around Arévalo The most popular hiking route is Arévalo River Walk, an easy 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This path follows the river, offering a flat and accessible experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route connects Arévalo with Fuente el Saúz, traversing open countryside. Local hikers also love the Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through pine areas, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Arévalo is defined by river paths, trails connecting towns, and open rural landscapes. The network offers options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Arévalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a small number of reviews. More than 1 hiker has used komoot to explore Arévalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical length of hikes in Arévalo?
A: Hikes in Arévalo vary in length, with options ranging from shorter paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; at 2.5 miles (4.1 km) to longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 miles (12.5 km).

Q: Are there easy hiking trails in Arévalo?
A: Yes, Arévalo offers several easy hiking trails characterized by gentle terrain and minimal elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256166544/arevalo-to-fuente-el-sauz-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo to Fuente el Saúz Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) option, both suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Arévalo?
A: Absolutely. The region's gentle terrain and river paths make many trails suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146251197/arevalo-river-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo River Walk&lt;/a&gt; is particularly family-friendly, offering a flat and accessible experience along the river.

Q: Can I find circular walks in Arévalo?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 7.8-mile (12.5 km) loop through pine areas. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146324807/from-tinosillos-to-el-bohodon-circular-by-the-camino-de-el-chorrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Arévalo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Arévalo, especially those traversing open countryside and river paths. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash and respect local regulations, particularly when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Arévalo?
A: Hiking in Arévalo is characterized by river paths, open rural landscapes, and routes connecting local towns. You'll experience gentle terrain, often passing through fields and along waterways, providing a peaceful natural and rural environment.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Arévalo is rich in historical and natural attractions. You can explore sites like the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arévalo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5856915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5768284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arevalillo Riverbank&lt;/a&gt; itself is a pleasant natural feature often integrated into walks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Arévalo?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Arévalo, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the region's gentle terrain, the peaceful river paths, and the opportunity to explore the quiet rural landscapes and historical towns.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Arévalo?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead may be limited, Arévalo is a town with local bus services that can connect you to various points within the municipality and nearby villages. From these points, many trails, particularly those connecting towns, are accessible on foot.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes in Arévalo?
A: Yes, beyond the easy routes, Arévalo also offers moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248233708/ermita-del-cristo-de-los-pinares-from-nava-de-arevalo-circular-by-pedro-rodriguez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo de los Pinares from Nava de Arévalo — circular by Pedro Rodríguez&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146324807/from-tinosillos-to-el-bohodon-circular-by-the-camino-de-el-chorrillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Tiñosillos to El Bohodón — circular by the Camino de El Chorrillo&lt;/a&gt; are examples of moderate routes that provide a slightly longer and more engaging experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arévalo?
A: The best time to hike in Arévalo is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable walking conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate gear.
tips: The Arevalillo River is a watercourse that flows through the interior of the Iberian Peninsula. Its route is picturesque and crosses several municipalities in the province of Ávila, including Arévalo. Point close to the Medina Bridge, also known as Puente Llana, is a medieval jewel that rises majestically over the river.

In the 11th century, during the reign of Alfonso VI, a charter was granted to the town of Arévalo. From then on, documentary references abound about this place. Although it had periods of lordly rule, it generally depended on the Crown (being a royal estate), which explains the presence of members of the royal family in the town.

Plaza de la Villa is one of the most beautiful squares in Spain. Its architecture transports us to the Middle Ages, when it was the epicenter of social and economic life in Arévalo.
It is surrounded by buildings with arches, built with stone, wood and brick. The floor is cobbled, adding to its charm.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The circular route is approximately 4.08 km (about 2.5 miles) long. Most hikers complete it in around 1 hour and 5 minutes, though a leisurely pace might extend it to about 2.5 hours.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is an easy hike, considered suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families with children. The paths are easily accessible, making it ideal for a leisurely stroll.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the trailhead are not detailed, the trail is a circular route around Arévalo and is often referred to as the 'Paseo Fluvial de Arévalo' or 'Cinturón Verde' (Green Belt). Parking can generally be found within the town of Arévalo, close to the riverbanks or the historic center, allowing easy access to various points along the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Arévalo y Ribera Río Arevalillo — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of diverse wildlife and other hikers along the riverside paths.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers views of the historic Arévalo Castle, the Arevalillo Riverbank, and the confluence of the Arevalillo and Adaja rivers. You'll also encounter iron sculptures by local artist Juan Jesús Villaverde, the rehabilitated Molino de Don Álvaro de Luna, and sections of the 'Route of the Eleven Bridges' with historic structures like Puente de Medina and Puente de los Lobos.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery along the riverbanks. The riverside forest provides shade during warmer months, and the flat terrain is generally manageable even after rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular route. It is a public fluvial promenade and green belt accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat and easy, following the banks of the Arevalillo and Adaja rivers. You'll walk through a dense, almost wild riverside forest, with easily accessible paths. The region's topography is a flat plain, making for a comfortable and leisurely walk.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino de Santiago del Sureste (51% of the route), Etapa 24: Arévalo - Medina del Campo (18%), and is also known as the Paseo fluvial (51%).

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or specific spots for good photos of the castle?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail provides spectacular and unique views of the Arévalo Castle from various vantage points, especially as it borders the western flank of the castle. The riverside paths offer excellent opportunities for photography.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail circles the town of Arévalo, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs within the town center, particularly near the Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín, which is also a highlight along the route. These establishments offer convenient options for refreshments before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Plaza de la Villa and Church of San Martín, Arévalo
